metadata.title: Cookies for the lunar landing watch party
metadata.curiosityNote: People trace constellations by connecting bright stars into remembered patterns.

story.briefTemplate: The principal brought {quantity:star_cookies_start} for students to enjoy at the NASA lunar landing watch party. Before guests arrived, the middle school classes used {quantity:constellation_cookies} to build a cookie constellation map on blue paper. The science teacher brought an additional tray of {quantity:rocket_cookies}.
story.causalEvent: The middle school classes moved some cookies into a constellation map.
story.closingNoteTemplate: The cookie log closed with {quantity:total_party_cookies} ready for the watch party.

label.child: Cookies used for the constellation map
Star cookies still ready to eat
Rocket cookies the science teacher brought
Cookies ready for the watch party

steps.prompt: How many star cookies were still ready to eat after the constellation map was built?
steps.reasoningPrompt: After the middle school classes moved cookies into the constellation map, did the ready-to-eat star cookie count go up or down?

backwardCheck.prompt: Move the constellation cookies back. Do you reach the starting tray?

steps.prompt: How many cookies were ready for the watch party altogether?
steps.reasoningPrompt: The remaining star cookies and the rocket cookies are both ready for the party. How do they make the total?

backwardCheck.prompt: Take the rocket cookies back out. Do you reach the ready star cookies?

operatorExperiments.alternateWorldTemplate: Adding would fit a different story where {quantity:constellation_cookies} more cookies were slid onto the tray instead.
This matches the story: the classes moved {quantity:constellation_cookies} into the constellation map, so they leave the tray.
This would fit a different question: if there were {value:constellation_cookies} trays like {label:star_cookies_start.lowercase}, how many would there be?
This would fit a different question about sharing {label:star_cookies_start.lowercase} into equal groups.
This matches the story: {label:star_cookies_remaining.lowercase} and {label:rocket_cookies.lowercase} are set out together.
Subtracting would fit a different story where {quantity:rocket_cookies} were carried away instead of added.
This would fit a different question: if there were {value:rocket_cookies} groups like {label:star_cookies_remaining.lowercase}, how many would there be?
This would fit a different question about sharing {label:star_cookies_remaining.lowercase} into equal groups.

recap.headline: Why the watch party had {quantity:total_party_cookies}
causalChain: The classes used {quantity:constellation_cookies} for the constellation map.

dataQuestion.prompt: What does {quantity:constellation_cookies} represent in the first model?
dataQuestion.correctFeedback: Right. {quantity:constellation_cookies} is the part the classes moved into the constellation map.
dataQuestion.incorrectFeedback: That amount is the part moved into the constellation map: {quantity:constellation_cookies}.
